project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS Uttara Kanda moment corpus
designPillars: 10 Uttara Kanda moments derived from ELGODS source citations
Valmiki sarga citations preserved per moment
Voice cue IDs follow Day 13 register pattern vc_uttara_<verb>_<id>

moments.narrative: I place Rama's sandals on the throne. For 14 years, I govern in his name. The sandals rule, not Bharata. Every decree is checked against the brother who is not here. The kingdom learns the meaning of trust.
moments.moralLesson: A true king serves the rightful king even when denied the crown

moments.narrative: Fourteen years alone. Fear made me the villain. Fear of losing everything. Which is exactly what I caused. Bharata will not forgive me. The kingdom whispers. I sit in the empty palace and wait for the return I demanded.
moments.moralLesson: Fear-driven ambition destroys the very thing it seeks

moments.narrative: Mother! You killed father! I will never forgive you! Bharata's words echo through the palace corridors. Kaikeyi hears them every morning. She does not answer. She cannot. Some griefs have no rebuttal.
moments.moralLesson: A mother's act can echo as a son's lifelong wound

moments.narrative: Fire. Hanuman has set Lanka alight. The women of the palace weep. Trijata tells me this is not destruction — it is reckoning. Mandodari stands in the ash and dismisses the last illusion: Ravana is not invincible.
moments.moralLesson: Even in defeat, clarity can replace delusion

moments.narrative: A citizen questions Sita's purity. Rama does not argue. He asks Sita to prove herself. She agrees. She walks into the fire. The fire does not burn her. The gods witness. She returns. But Rama exiles her anyway — for the kingdom's silence must be honored.
moments.moralLesson: A king's duty can wound his own heart

moments.narrative: Rama asks me to take Sita to the forest. Pregnant. Alone. This is the hardest thing I have ever done. I take her to the ashram. I build the hut. I stay until the children are born. Then I return to Ayodhya with an empty heart.
moments.moralLesson: Duty carried in silence is still duty

moments.narrative: Sixty years. The ascetics laugh. Sometimes I doubt. But the waiting itself is prayer. Shabari sits in the ashram and watches the road. She has no feet to walk to Ayodhya, but her heart arrived before Bharata's sandals.
moments.moralLesson: Faith without proof is still faith

moments.narrative: Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana in the court of Ayodhya. They are twins, born in the forest, raised by Valmiki. Rama weeps. Sita watches from the palace balcony. The song ends. She descends into the earth that bore her.

I call upon my mother — the Earth herself. Take me home. The ground splits open. A great throne rises. She descends. The sky darkens. Rama runs to the edge and holds out his hands. The earth does not give her back.

Rama weeps at the edge of the earth. He has won the war. He has won the kingdom. He has lost the woman who walked into fire for him. The ashram is empty. The forest is empty. The earth has taken back its daughter.
moments.moralLesson: Victory can be the loneliest place
